items.title: Scammer Nabbed After Collecting RM30 Ward Admission ‘Fee’ At HSAJB
items.summary: A woman has been arrested after allegedly impersonating as a staff member at HSAJB and collecting an RM30 ward admission "fee" from patients or their next-of-kin. The Johor hospital tells the public to make payments only at authorised payment counters. The post Scammer Nabbed After Collecting RM30 Ward Admission ‘Fee’ At HSAJB appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: NHMS 2025: Malaysia Must Accelerate Healthy Ageing Reforms — Malaysian Society of Geriatric Medicine & Gerontological Association of Malaysia
items.summary: NHMS 2025 findings strongly reinforce the importance of accelerating implementation of Malaysia’s Pelan Tindakan Perkhidmatan Kesihatan Warga Emas 2023–2030. Healthy ageing isn't just about living longer but maintaining function, dignity, and independence. The post NHMS 2025: Malaysia Must Accelerate Healthy Ageing Reforms — Malaysian Society of Geriatric Medicine & Gerontological Association of Malaysia appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Healthy Ageing In Malaysia: Strengthening Public Health Capacity And Long-Term Preventive Strategies — Assistant Medical Officer
items.summary: Without decisive intervention now, Malaysia risks facing a future where increased life expectancy does not translate into quality of life, but prolonged years of illness and dependency. The post Healthy Ageing In Malaysia: Strengthening Public Health Capacity And Long-Term Preventive Strategies — Assistant Medical Officer appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Predatory Audiology Practices Fuel Malaysia’s Dementia Crisis — Dr Bryan Kek
items.summary: If we do not regulate the cost and quality of hearing health today, our health care system will pay a much higher price for dementia wards tomorrow. The post Predatory Audiology Practices Fuel Malaysia’s Dementia Crisis — Dr Bryan Kek appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Malaysia Short Of Nearly 6,000 Vets, Says DVS
items.summary: Malaysia is facing a severe shortage of nearly 6,000 veterinarians, says the DVS, as the country only has 2,600 active vets compared to 8,500 needed. The ratio of vets to people in Malaysia is 1:13,700, far worse than the international standard of 1:5,000. The post Malaysia Short Of Nearly 6,000 Vets, Says DVS appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Selangor Expects Up To 25% Medical Cost Hike
items.summary: Selangor MB Amirudin Shari expects medical costs to increase by 15% to 25% amid the Persian Gulf conflict, noting that nearly 70% to 80% of medicines for Malaysia are sourced from overseas. The post Selangor Expects Up To 25% Medical Cost Hike appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: No Look-Back, No Way Forward? Why Malaysia’s Insurance Reform Still Leaves HIV Behind — Mohamad Zakifikri Mohamad Nor
items.summary: The “no look-back” clause asks insurers to stop looking into the past. But if HIV remains excluded, Malaysia’s insurance system is still looking away from the future. The post No Look-Back, No Way Forward? Why Malaysia’s Insurance Reform Still Leaves HIV Behind — Mohamad Zakifikri Mohamad Nor appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: MOH Probe Clears UMMC Over Cancer ‘Cluster’: Mosti
items.summary: Mosti says a probe by MOH's medical radiation regulatory division didn't find radiation safety breaches that could be linked to an alleged cancer cluster at UMMC. The regulator of radiation for medical purposes is the Health DG. The post MOH Probe Clears UMMC Over Cancer ‘Cluster’: Mosti appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Only 15% Of Malaysian Seniors Ageing Well, Dementia Rate Up: NHMS 2025
items.summary: NHMS 2025 reveals a poor state of health among Malaysian seniors: only 15% ageing well, dementia prevalence rose to 10%, and high prevalence of NCDs (diabetes: 39%, hypertension: 73%, high cholesterol: 76%). About 68% live with at least 2 chronic diseases. The post Only 15% Of Malaysian Seniors Ageing Well, Dementia Rate Up: NHMS 2025 appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Corporate Benefit Schemes Can’t Override Doctors’ Clinical Decisions, Says MMA
items.summary: MMA says corporate benefit schemes cannot interfere with doctors' clinical decisions, stressing there's a clear boundary between what's covered by employers and what's prescribed by doctors based on clinical needs. MMA urges MOH to issue clear guidance. The post Corporate Benefit Schemes Can’t Override Doctors’ Clinical Decisions, Says MMA appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: MyUBAT app can verify all drug holograms, Health Ministry says amid safety concerns
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 22 — The Ministry of Health Malaysia (MOH) has refuted claims of a failure in the verification sy...
